title: Backtracking & Search Algorithms
concept: Implementing depth-first or breadth-first search to backtrack when a branch hits a dead end.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: what does a validator model do in Tree of Thoughts?

options.text: It scores each candidate step as promising, questionable, or a dead-end
It writes the final answer without checking anything
It deletes all the ideas the AI came up with

exercises.explain: A validator model judges each step and labels it (like promising or dead-end) so the AI knows which paths are worth continuing.

cards.title: Lost in a maze?
cards.body: Imagine walking a maze. You try a path, hit a wall, then walk back to the last fork and try a different way. That walking-back move is called backtracking.

cards.title: Backtracking + Search
cards.body: In Tree of Thoughts, the AI explores branches of ideas. When a branch is scored a dead-end, the AI backtracks to an earlier step and searches a different branch. A search algorithm decides the order it explores.

cards.title: Breadcrumbs on the trail
cards.body: Like leaving breadcrumbs so you can find your way back, the AI remembers each fork it passed. If a path fails, it returns to a remembered fork and tries an unexplored option.

cards.title: Solving a number puzzle
cards.body: The AI tries adding first, hits a dead-end, and backtracks. It returns to the start and tries multiplying instead. Backtracking lets it recover instead of giving up.

cards.title: Not just guessing again
cards.body: Myth: backtracking means starting over from scratch. Truth: it only steps back to the last good fork, keeping all the progress before it.

cards.title: Two ways to search
cards.body: Depth-first dives deep down one branch first; breadth-first checks all nearby options before going deeper.

left.text: Follow one path all the way down, then backtrack if it dead-ends.

right.text: Check every option at the current level before going deeper.

exercises.prompt: A branch of ideas is scored a dead-end. What does backtracking do?

options.text: Returns to an earlier fork and tries a different branch
Keeps forcing the same dead-end branch forward
Erases the whole tree and starts from nothing
Picks a random word as the final answer

exercises.explain: Backtracking steps back to the last good fork and explores a fresh branch, saving earlier progress instead of restarting.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Depth-first search follows one branch deep before backtracking.
exercises.explain: Depth-first search dives down a single path as far as it can, and only backtracks when that path hits a dead-end.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about recovering from a dead-end.
exercises.sentence: When a branch fails, the AI uses backtracking to return to an earlier fork.
exercises.explain: Backtracking is the move that sends the AI back to a previous good fork so it can try another branch.

exercises.prompt: Which explanation of search order sounds AI-generated (smooth and complete)?

options.text: Breadth-first search systematically evaluates every option at the current level before descending to explore deeper branches of the tree.
bfs go wide then down idk
breadth first... you check the close ones first? then go deeper maybe

exercises.explain: The AI-written option is polished, uses full sentences, and precise words like 'systematically' and 'descending' rather than casual filler.

exercises.prompt: An AI is solving a puzzle with Tree of Thoughts. It dives deep down one branch, the validator scores it a dead-end, and it steps back to the last fork to try another path. Which search style and move is this?

options.text: Erasing the tree and starting over

exercises.explain: Diving deep down one branch first is depth-first search, and stepping back to the last good fork after a dead-end is backtracking. Together they let the AI explore efficiently and recover from wrong paths without losing all its progress.
